judge_reason: Description B incorrectly states all three lines peak near 100% breakup, while Description A correctly describes different peak heights (near 100%, 100%, and 80-85%) for the three lines.
original_image_tags: A line graph titled "Atlas IIAS". The y-axis is "Breakup Percent (%)" ranging from 0 to 100 in increments of 10. The x-axis is "Failure Time (sec)" ranging from 0 to 280 in increments of 40. The graph contains three curves representing different "q-alpha in deg-lb/ft²": a solid line for "q-alpha = 5,000", a long-dashed line for "q-alpha = 10,000", and a short-dashed line for "q-alpha = 20,000". All three curves start near 0% at 0 seconds, rise sharply to peaks between 40-120 seconds (with the 5,000 curve peaking highest near 100%, followed by the 10,000 curve, and the 20,000 curve peaking lowest near 80-85%), and then decline back towards 0% by 280 seconds. The curves intersect and vary in relative position as failure time increases.
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: A black-and-white line chart titled “Atlas IIAS,” plotting Breakup Percent (%) versus Failure Time (sec) from 0 to 280, with three curves labeled q-alpha = 5,000, 10,000, and 20,000 deg-lb/ft².*
